title: Asagov Raider

The '''''Asagov Raider''''', formerly known as the '''''Cekbar Servant''''', was a [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[Action IV transport]] operating during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Originally a [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] registered to [[Nalroni/Legends|Nalroni]] [[Consortium of Nalroni merchants|businessmen]] on the trading [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Celanon/Legends|Celanon]], the ship was captured by the [[Saurton]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Miktiss]] around [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. Miktiss had the vessel heavily modified and renamed as the ''Asagov Raider'', using the medium [[Bulk freighter/Legends|bulk freighter]] as his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] and as the command base of the [[Des'maric Pirates]]. The ''Asagov Raider'' was a mobile headquarters for the organization while they operated in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].

==Description==
The ''Asagov Raider'' was a [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[Action IV transport]].<ref name="tdc" /> Like other Action IVs, the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] was trapezoidal in shape<ref name="sotg01">''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]''</ref> and measured 100 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length.<ref name="tdc" /> Although standard models came unarmed,<ref name="sotg01" /> the ''Asagov Raider'' was heavily modified to serve as a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] vessel by the [[Saurton]] [[Miktiss]]. The ship boasted eight [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] and twelve double [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]. The ion cannons were placed evenly around the medium [[Bulk freighter/Legends|bulk freighter]] for maximum coverage, while the [[Laser/Legends|laser]] cannons were concentrated to the fore, with some weapons covering the port and starboard fire arcs. Other modifications included stronger [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]], increased [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] speed, and [[Detention area|detention cells]] on the lower level.<ref name="tdc" />

The ship was equipped with a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]] for [[Astrogation/Legends|plotting]] [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jumps, and a Class 3 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], with a Class 15 as a backup. Crewed by fifteen beings, the ''Asagov Raider'' also required a complement of forty [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] to operate effectively. A skeleton crew of eight personnel and ten gunners could run the ship, but at a cost of efficiency. The vessel could carry 60,000 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] of cargo, and store consumables for the crew to last 3 [[Standard month/Legends|months]].<ref name="tdc" />

==History==
[[File:Saurton6.jpg|thumb|left|100px|Miktiss, leader of the Des'maric Pirates]]
Commissioned as a medium bulk freighter, the ''Cekbar Servant'' was originally owned by a [[Consortium of Nalroni merchants|consortium]] of [[Nalroni/Legends|Nalroni]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Celanon/Legends|Celanon]],<ref name="tdc" /> a major trade world in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="pog01">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume One]]''</ref> Around [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="date">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' dates itself to four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which equates to [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the Saurton pirate Miktiss, head of the [[Des'maric Pirates]], captured the vessel shortly after acquiring a [[Des'maric Blastboats|trio]] of [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat|Skipray Blastboats]] from the planet [[Elshandruu Pica]]. Realizing that, in its current state, it was not a vessel fit for piracy, Miktiss forced a former [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] [[Unidentified ship engineer|engineer]]—through a combination of blackmail and bargaining—to modify the ship to his specifications. The [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] complied, fitting the ship out with extra weapons, stronger [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] generators, and a faster sublight drive.<ref name="tdc" />

Renamed as the "''Asagov Raider''," the ship served as a mobile [[Flagship/Legends|command base]] for the Des'maric Pirates while they operated within the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]<ref name="tdc" /> around [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].<ref name="date" /> Despite being a formidable raiding vessel, the ship was generally kept away from combat areas to keep it safe. Although fully capable of subduing merchant [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] and similar vessels, a single [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] or escort [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] could have easily destroyed the vessel.<ref name="tdc" />

==Commanders and crew==
Crewed by some of the less-skilled members of the organization—Miktiss regularly assigned his best pirates to the three Skipray Blastboats that would see actual combat—the vessel was known for its loose discipline. However, during combat situations, the personnel stationed on board were capable of performing like a military crew.<ref name="tdc" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Action_IV.jpg|thumb|right|An Action IV transport, of the same class as the ''Raider'']]
The ''Asagov Raider''{{'s}} only appearance has been in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]], where it is described as the flagship and command base of the Des'maric Pirates. The vessel is never encountered in any of the adventures, being detailed only in the "Rogues Gallery" section of the book, written by [[Bill Smith]]. The inclusion of the vessel is to allow the gamemaster to use it in an original adventure.<ref name="tdc" />

Under its original name, "''Cekbar Servant''," the vessel appears to be named for the continent of [[Cekbar]] on the planet Celanon. When it was renamed by Miktiss, the vessel appears to have taken its name from the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Asagov]], a settlement on the Saurton [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Essowyn]].<ref name="pog01" />
